summary: Scans the docs/ folder, identifies all PDF files, and writes a manifest.json that records each document's path, doc_id, doc_type (statute/constitution/judgment), and enabled status. Acts as the pipeline's source of truth.
Reads manifest.json, opens each enabled PDF with pypdf, and extracts text page-by-page. Outputs raw_pages.json with per-page text and page numbers preserved for traceability.
Removes front matter, noise pages, and headers from raw extracted text. Detects the true body start page for each document and outputs structured_documents.json ready for chunking.

The core chunking module. Routes each document to a type-specific strategy: statutes are split at section boundaries, the Constitution at article boundaries, and judgments by paragraph blocks with a 3-paragraph sliding window (1 overlap). Uses regex patterns tuned for legal text. Validates minimum chunk length and deduplicates by chunk_id.

Runs quality checks over chunks.json: validates chunk_id format, checks page range consistency, flags suspiciously short chunks, and reports any metadata schema violations. Acts as a pre-embedding gate.
Reads chunks.json, generates embeddings using OpenAI text-embedding-3-small, and stores vectors + full metadata in a persistent ChromaDB collection named 'legal_rag' under db/.

Three-tier retrieval engine. First attempts exact reference matching (regex-extracts section/article number from query, does direct metadata filter). Then runs semantic vector search from ChromaDB. Falls back to lexical TF scoring with domain-specific boosts if ChromaDB is unavailable. Has a built-in 10-case evaluation suite with PASS/FAIL reporting.

Assembles the final prompt from retrieved chunks and sends to Groq llama-3.3-70b-versatile. System prompt enforces a strict no-hallucination policy. Returns 'I do not have information regarding this.' when retrieval is insufficient. CLI supports single-query and interactive loop modes.
